title: Self Driving Products: Product Signals to Pull Requests — Joshua Snyder, PostHog
summary: PostHog is building an automated pipeline that detects product signals like rage clicks, error spikes, and customer messages, then uses a background agent to group related signals, research the codebase, and generate pull requests. The system automatically surfaces fixes to developers without requiring manual triage and ticketing.
writeup: This talk from PostHog's Joshua Snyder describes an automated pipeline that converts product signals into pull requests without manual triage or ticketing. The system detects signals like rage clicks, error spikes, and customer messages, then runs a background agent that groups related signals, researches the codebase, and generates fixes for developers. The flow draws on agentic workflows, clustering, embeddings, contrastive learning, and test-time compute. The takeaway is a concrete pattern for surfacing candidate fixes directly to developers rather than queuing work for human review.
